title: Feat/add force capability on export
body:
This PR introduces a --force / -f flag to the marimo export subcommands. This allows users to overwrite existing output files, which is essential for automation and scripting use cases.

Fixes #5523

Currently, when marimo export is used with an --output argument that points to an existing file, it interactively prompts the user for confirmation before overwriting. While this is a safe default for manual use, it blocks non-interactive workflows, such as in CI/CD pipelines or automated scripts.

This PR resolves the issue by:
- Adding a --force and -f option to all relevant export commands (html, script, md, ipynb, and html_wasm).
- Updating the watch_and_export function to check for this flag. If force is True, the prompt_to_overwrite confirmation step is skipped, and the file is overwritten directly.
- Adding a new pytest test case (test_cli_export_html_force_overwrite) to ensure the new functionality works as expected. The test verifies that:
  - Attempting to export to an existing file without the flag does not overwrite it.
  - Using --force successfully overwrites the existing file.
  - Using the short alias -f also works correctly.

These changes make the export feature more versatile and friendly for automated environments without altering the safe default behavior for interactive users.

I know it is not perfect, please @dmadisetti @mscolnick check this one out!

step_plan.summary: Add a global --force/-f flag across all marimo export subcommands to enable overwriting existing outputs in non-interactive environments, while preserving the safe interactive defaults. Implement force-aware logic in the watch/export pipeline and propagate the flag through all per-format export commands.

steps.operation: Extend watch_and_export signature to accept a global force flag
steps.reason: Introduce a global force: bool parameter so all export paths (one-shot and watch) can opt to overwrite existing output files non-interactively.
steps.side_effects: Requires updating all call sites and tests; potential API surface changes for external scripts using the export flow.

steps.operation: Add forced overwrite support in the main overwrite flow
steps.reason: Modify the overwrite gate to skip interactive prompts when force is True and proceed to write, enabling non-interactive automation.
steps.side_effects: Risk of unintended data loss if force is misused in scripts; update docs and CLI help to clarify usage.

steps.operation: Enable write_data to honor force and perform force-driven overwrites
steps.reason: Centralize file writing behavior under force mode so both initial exports and re-exports in watch mode overwrite as requested.
steps.side_effects: Tests relying on write behavior may need updates; ensure file writes are atomic if applicable.

steps.operation: Make on_file_changed respect force for re-exports in watch mode
steps.reason: Ensure file-change events trigger overwrites consistently when force is enabled during watch mode.
steps.side_effects: May affect event handling logic; verify no race conditions with concurrent writes.

steps.operation: Propagate force through the startup/watch loop
steps.reason: Pass the force flag into the asynchronous watch loop so initial export and subsequent re-exports respect non-interactive overwrite semantics.
steps.side_effects: Watch lifecycle behavior may interact with filesystem events; ensure shutdown/restart paths remain stable.

steps.operation: Html export: add --force flag and thread it into watch_and_export
steps.reason: Extend HTML export command to support non-interactive overwrites for automation scripts.
steps.side_effects: CLI help/docs updated; consistent behavior across all export targets.

steps.operation: Script export: add --force flag and thread it into watch_and_export
steps.reason: Extend Script export command to support non-interactive overwrites for automation scripts.
steps.side_effects: CLI help/docs updated; ensure no regressions in interactive usage.

steps.operation: Markdown export: add --force flag and thread it into watch_and_export
steps.reason: Extend MD export command to support non-interactive overwrites for automation scripts.
steps.side_effects: Docs and tests may require updates; maintain consistent behavior with other formats.

steps.operation: IPYNB export: add --force flag and thread it into watch_and_export
steps.reason: Extend IPYNB export command to support non-interactive overwrites for automation scripts.
steps.side_effects: Ensure outputs overwrite correctly; verify memory/project notebook generation paths.

steps.operation: HTML_WASM export: add --force flag and thread it into watch_and_export
steps.reason: Extend WASM export command to support non-interactive overwrites for automation scripts.
steps.side_effects: Ensure consistency with other export targets; update help/docs accordingly.
